messages.content:
You are itsy, working under an active contract. Working directory: /app/personal-site.
Model: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF:IQ2_XXS

## Active contract: Find and merge lost personal site changes into master (active)
Assertions: 0 passed · 0 failed · 4 pending · 0 skipped (of 4)

  [ ] A.001  The lost changes are located (identified branch, stash, or uncommitted work)
  [ ] A.002  The lost changes are successfully merged into the master branch
  [ ] A.003  All modified files from the lost changes appear in the working tree on master
  [ ] A.004  git status on master is clean (no uncommitted changes after merge)


How to work:
- ONE tool call per response. Reason only about the immediate next step — not the full solution.
- After each tool call, stop. Wait for the result. Then decide the next single action.
- Focus on the FIRST pending assertion. Ignore the others for now.
- Look at the most recent tool result. What is the single most direct action to move toward passing it?
- Do the work for each pending assertion (write_file / patch / bash — all available now).
- When you've verified an assertion, call `mark_assertion` with:
id          the assertion id (A.001, A.002, …)
state       "passed" / "failed" / "skipped"
evidence    one-sentence summary of how you verified
command     (recommended for passed) the shell command you ran
exit_code   the exit code
observation the actual output you saw — NOT "OK" or "passed"
- When every assertion is `passed`, call `close_contract completed` to finish.
- `close_contract completed` is refused until every assertion is `passed`.
- Assertions can only be `passed` or `failed` — there is no skip or abort.

Define what 'done' means for the following task by calling propose_contract. Do not start the task yet — just enumerate the checks that would prove it's done.

TASK:
I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.

messages.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to help them find some changes they made to their personal site that are no longer visible after checking out master. This sounds like they might have been working on a different branch, or perhaps stashed their changes, or maybe they committed on a different branch.

I need to first understand what's in the workspace and then propose a contract for finding and merging those changes. But per my instructions, I MUST call propose_contract first before anything else.

Let me think about what "done" means here:
1. Find the lost changes (could be on another branch, in stash, or in uncommitted work)
2. Merge/apply those changes into master

I should propose a contract that captures:
- The lost changes are found
- The changes are successfully merged into master
- The result is verifiable (the changes are present in master)

Let me propose the contract first.

function.arguments: {"assertions":[{"id": "A.001", "text": "The lost changes are located (identified branch, stash, or uncommitted work)"}, {"id": "A.002", "text": "The lost changes are successfully merged into the master branch"}, {"id": "A.003", "text": "All modified files from the lost changes appear in the working tree on master"}, {"id": "A.004", "text": "git status on master is clean (no uncommitted changes after merge)"}],"brief":"Find the user's lost changes that disappeared after checking out master, then merge them back into master. The changes could be on a different branch, in git stash, or as uncommitted local modifications. Once found, apply/merge them into master so the user's work is preserved.","title":"Find and merge lost personal site changes into master"}
function.name: propose_contract

messages.content: Contract `find-and-merge-lost-personal-sit-20260524-054143` created with 4 assertions. Work the assertions, marking each with mark_assertion as you go. Call close_contract("completed") when every assertion is non-pending.

messages.reasoning_content:
Interesting! I can see the reflog shows:
- `HEAD@{1}`: commit "Move to Stanford" at `650dba4` - this is on a detached HEAD state
- `HEAD@{0}`: checkout from `650dba427e0a9dcd118f41a4c5e35c8017550a5a` to master

So the user was on a detached HEAD at commit `650dba4` which had a commit "Move to Stanford", then they checked out master. The changes from that commit are lost!

Let me look at what that commit contains.

I found the lost changes! The commit `650dba4` on detached HEAD had "Move to Stanford" which modified `_includes/about.md` and `_layouts/default.html`. I need to cherry-pick this commit onto master to restore those changes.

Let me do that now.

The goal is to apply the "Move to Stanford" changes, so I should take the incoming changes (theirs) for the parts that were changed in the lost commit.

cd /app/personal-site && cat _includes/about.md
<<<<<<< HEAD
I am a sixth PhD candidate at the [Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering](https://www.cs.washington.edu/) at The University of Washington, where I am advised by [Tim Althoff](http://www.timalthoff.com/). I am affiliated with [UW NLP](https://twitter.com/uwnlp).

 Previously, I was a Student Researcher at [Google Research](https://research.google/), an ML Research Intern at [Apple Health AI](https://machinelearning.apple.com/work-with-us) and a data scientist and the second full-time employee at [HealthRhythms](https://www.healthrhythms.com). 
=======
I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS.

Previously, I was a Student Researcher at [Google Research](https://research.google/), an ML Research Intern at [Apple Health AI](https://machinelearning.apple.com/work-with-us) and a data scientist and the second full-time employee at [HealthRhythms](https://www.healthrhythms.com). 
>>>>>>> 650dba4 (Move to Stanford)
printf '\n__ITSY_END_03de6125ba72c44c_%d_\n' $?

Let me resolve by keeping the "theirs" (incoming) content.

function.description: Search the code graph for a symbol, function, or class name. Returns connected code with context. Use for "how does X work" or "find the auth logic" — NOT for listing projects.
function.name: graph_search

query.description: Symbol name or concept to search for

function.description: Load relevant project memory for a task. Returns past decisions, workflows, conventions, and gotchas. Call this before starting complex work.
function.name: memory_load

task.description: Task description to find relevant context for

function.description: Read a file. Returns content with line numbers.
function.name: read_file

function.description: Create or overwrite a file. LIMIT: 60 lines / 8KB max. For larger files write a skeleton then use patch to add sections.
function.name: write_file

function.description: Edit a file by replacing old_str with new_str. old_str must match exactly ONE location.
function.name: patch

function.description: Run a shell command. Returns stdout/stderr.
function.name: bash

function.description: Search file contents using regex (ripgrep). Returns matching lines.
function.name: search

function.description: Find files matching a glob pattern.
function.name: find_files

function.description: Save durable knowledge to project memory. Only save facts that should persist: decisions, workflows, gotchas, conventions. NOT task transcripts.
function.name: memory_remember
propose_contract

brief.description: 1-3 paragraph plan: what you'll do, the constraints, the shape of done.

features.description: Optional sub-tasks. Each can fulfill one or more assertion IDs.

function.description: Mark a contract assertion `passed` or `failed` with evidence. `passed` requires evidence + at least one verification command (the actual output you saw). Lazy observations like "OK" or "tests passed" are rejected — write what you actually saw.
function.name: mark_assertion

command.description: Command you ran (optional but encouraged for `passed`)

evidence.description: Plain-text explanation of how you verified (≥10 chars)

function.description: Mark a contract feature `in_progress` / `done` / `cancelled`. Optional bookkeeping; not required.
function.name: mark_feature

function.description: Read the current contract state — which assertions are passed / failed / pending.
function.name: contract_status

function.description: Finalize the active contract as `completed`. Refused unless every assertion is `passed`.
function.name: close_contract

function.description: Read a file, then apply a patch to it in one step. Equivalent to read_file + patch but in a single tool call.
function.name: read_and_patch

function.description: Create a file and then run a command (like running the file or running tests). Equivalent to write_file + bash in one call.
function.name: create_and_run

function.description: Find files matching a pattern and read the first match. Equivalent to find_files + read_file in one call.
function.name: find_and_read

read_lines.description: Max lines to show from matched file. Default: 50

function.description: Search for a pattern in code, then read the most relevant file found. Equivalent to search + read_file in one call.
function.name: search_and_read

read_context.description: Lines of context around matches. Default: 10

message.reasoning_content: The cherry-pick is complete. The working tree is clean, and the "Move to Stanford" commit is now on master. Let me verify the files were changed correctly by checking what was modified.
